result:
Done. I wrote `index.html` to the current directory — a single self-contained file you can double-click to open in any browser.

**What's in it:**

- **Left sidebar** — Northstar logo, two grouped nav sections (Dashboard active, Reports, Audience, Sessions / Integrations, Settings, Help), an "Upgrade to Pro" card, and a user profile footer.
- **Top bar** — page title, search input, notification bell with unread dot, and a "New Report" button. Includes a hamburger toggle that appears on small screens.
- **4 key metrics** — MRR, Active Users, Conversion Rate, Churn Rate, each with an icon and a colored up/down delta.
- **Chart area** — a hand-built SVG area chart (this-year vs last-year lines, gradient fill, gridlines, month labels) plus a donut "Traffic Sources" breakdown beside it.
- **Recent-activity list** — a live-feed style list with avatars, descriptions, and timestamps, alongside a "Top Pages" bar-progress panel.

**Technical notes:**
- Tailwind via CDN, Inter via Google Fonts — both allowed.
- No build step, no external images: the logo, charts, donut, and all icons are inline SVG.
- Dark theme, responsive (sidebar collapses below `lg`, metric/grid columns reflow), with subtle entrance animations on the metric cards.

Open it with `open index.html` (macOS) or just drag it into a browser tab.
